hero.eyebrow: Officially Appointed by the Embassy of Hungary
hero.headline: Honorary Consulate of Hungary
hero.tagline: Serving Hungarian citizens, dual nationals, and families across Maine, Vermont, New Hampshire, Rhode Island, and Massachusetts.
hero.trustCopy: Derry, NH · Monday Appointments · 5 States Served
hero.paymentNote: Cash & Check Only · In-Person · Appointment Required

items.headline: Documents that need to go to Hungary
items.body: A deed, a will, a bank document — and it must carry an official Hungarian consular seal before it's valid in Budapest.

items.headline: Uncertainty about the right process
items.body: Should you apostille it? Authenticate it? Go to the Embassy? The answer depends on the document — and getting it wrong means starting over.

items.headline: You shouldn't have to fly to New York
items.body: The Hungarian Consulate General in Manhattan handles what we can't. But most people don't need Manhattan — they need Derry, NH, which is actually reachable.

items.headline: Who do you even call?
items.body: Hospitals, universities, and courts in New England have no Hungarian-speaking liaison. For emergencies involving Hungarian citizens, Sylvia is the contact.

stats.zeroDrive: Miles to Drive to New York

feeTable.headline: Consular Fees
feeTable.intro: Fees are established by Hungarian Ministry decree. All services are in-person, by appointment, Monday only.
feeTable.savingsNote: A trip to the Consulate General in New York costs $80–$300+ in transportation alone — plus a full day of travel. The same consular authority, locally.
feeTable.paymentNote: Cash or check only. No credit cards accepted. Make checks payable to Sylvia Rich.

finalCTA.eyebrow: No Drive to New York Required
finalCTA.headline: You Don't Have to Drive to New York
finalCTA.body: The same consular authority. The same official seal. Right here in New Hampshire — for most of what you need.
